name: Sierro and Almanzora Greenway Loop
name_original: Castillo de Sierro — circular por la Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora
description: Cycle the moderate 15.5-mile Castillo de Sierro circular route via the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora, featuring historic castle views.
region_intro: Sierro Almería touring bike trails traverse a landscape characterized by the Sierra de los Filabres mountain range and the valley of the River Sierro. The region features varied terrain, including river valleys, mountainous backdrops, and the expansive Almanzora Valley. Dedicated cycling paths, such as the Vía Verde, utilize old train tracks, providing routes through scenic areas. This blend of natural features and historical infrastructure creates diverse touring cycling opportunities. Best touring cycling routes around Sierro The most popular touring cycling route is Sierro and Almanzora Greenway Loop, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This route follows a greenway through the valley landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Almanzora Valley Greenway Purchena Loop, a difficult 17.6 miles (28.3 km) path. It offers views of the Almanzora Valley and its greenway sections. Local touring cyclists also love the Old Tíjola train Station – Serón Railway Station loop from Tíjola, a 14.7 miles (23.7 km) trail leading through former railway lines, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Sierro is defined by the Sierra de los Filabres, the River Sierro valley, and the Almanzora Valley greenways. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Sierro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sierro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sierro?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes available around Sierro. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate to more challenging rides through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in Sierro?
A: The terrain around Sierro is characterized by a blend of mountain and valley landscapes. You'll find routes winding through the valley of the River Sierro, at the foothills of the Sierra de los Filabres mountain range, and across the expansive Almanzora Valley. Many routes utilize dedicated cycling paths, such as the Vía Verde, which repurpose old train tracks, offering a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Sierro?
A: The touring cycling routes in Sierro primarily cater to moderate and difficult ability levels. There are 9 moderate routes and 19 difficult routes, providing options for cyclists looking for a good challenge and scenic exploration.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Sierro area?
A: Yes, several routes in the Sierro area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047498286/sierro-and-almanzora-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierro and Almanzora Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a greenway through the valley, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Tíjola train Station – Serón Railway Station loop from Tíjola&lt;/a&gt;, which traces former railway lines.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling around Sierro?
A: While cycling around Sierro, you can expect to encounter diverse natural features. The routes often follow the River Sierro, known for its springs and pools. You'll also experience the mountainous backdrop of the Sierra de los Filabres and the scenic Almanzora Valley. Notable natural attractions include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Estrecho de Urrácal&lt;/a&gt; gorge with its unique geological formations, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Huevo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can integrate stops at landmarks like the Sierro Castle, an Islamic fortress from the 12th century offering panoramic views, and the neoclassical Church of San Sebastián. The Vía Verde routes also pass by historical railway stations such as the rehabilitated Serón Railway Station and the Old Tíjola train station, which are part of the Almanzora Valley greenway.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls or wild swimming spots near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the River Sierro, which winds through the municipality, features springs and pools that are perfect for cooling off. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Huevo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a specific natural highlight you might encounter near some routes, offering a picturesque spot for a break.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Sierro?
A: The touring cycling routes in Sierro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic greenways, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Sierro?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the Vía Verde sections, which repurpose old train tracks, often provide flatter and more accessible cycling experiences. These greenways can be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride through scenic areas, though specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly categorized.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sierro?
A: Sierro, located in Andalusia, Spain, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if cycling during this season. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some higher mountain passes might be colder.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes near Sierro?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the towns and villages that serve as starting points for the cycling routes, such as Sierro itself, Tíjola, or Serón. For routes utilizing the Vía Verde, look for parking facilities near the rehabilitated railway stations, which often serve as access points to these greenways.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Yes, as you cycle through the region, you'll pass through various towns and villages where you can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants for refreshments. Accommodation options are also available in these settlements, particularly in larger towns, allowing for multi-day touring experiences.
tips: Medieval castle-fortress of Islamic Berber style. With a difference of 300 m above the river that crosses the town of Sierro, this castle stands out for its prominence and good views of the entire territory that surrounds it. It dates from the 12th century and its reconstruction is from the year 2011.

I recommend that you invest a little while in visiting the town and its curious square dedicated to the moon. You can also recharge water and energy to continue the Route, if you are following the Greenway.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castillo de Sierro — circular via the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora route?
faqs.answer: The route combines sections of the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora, which is generally an easy path with gentle gradients, often paved or compacted gravel. However, as a 'moderate' circular route, expect some unpaved segments and a more demanding ascent to the Castillo de Sierro, which is situated at 755 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While portions along the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora are relatively easy and suitable for various fitness levels, the overall route is rated as 'moderate'. The circular aspect, particularly the climb to the Castillo de Sierro, introduces more challenging sections. Beginners with some cycling experience should be able to manage, but those new to cycling might find the ascents demanding.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You will encounter the historic Castillo de Sierro, a medieval Islamic fortress offering panoramic views over the Almanzora valley and surrounding towns like Somontín, Lúcar, and Urracal. The Vía Verde itself features old railway infrastructure such as tunnels and impressive viaducts. The route also passes through the charming settlement of Purchena.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. In winter, the area around the Castillo de Sierro can experience snow, and summer can be very hot, making cycling less comfortable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora for this route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the start of this exact circular route is not detailed, but access points to the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora are available in various towns along its length. You would typically look for parking in or near Sierro or other nearby villages that connect to the Vía Verde.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle on the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora or to visit the Castillo de Sierro. These are publicly accessible recreational paths and historical sites.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Sierro — circular via the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, Vías Verdes in Spain are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept on a leash. However, always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain, especially the more challenging sections leading to the Castillo de Sierro. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the Vía Verde passes through several charming villages, including Purchena, you will likely find cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and refresh. It's always a good idea to carry some water and snacks, especially for the sections between villages.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily utilizes the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora for a significant portion (37%). It also briefly intersects with other local paths such as Sierro por Boyarque (24%) and [CIMA AM08] Calar Alto * Tíjola (16%).

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and picturesque scenery. The Vía Verde winds through the Almanzora river valley, offering views of the Sierra de los Filabres and Sierra del Lucar mountain ranges. From the Castillo de Sierro, you'll get panoramic views of the high Almanzora valley. The route also passes through charming villages and over impressive viaducts.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the start of the route?
faqs.answer: While specific public transport details for Sierro are not readily available, smaller towns in rural Andalusia often have limited bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Sierro or nearby towns along the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora if you plan to use public transport.
